question: The pure Z_2 lattice gauge theory in four space-time dimensions (d=4) is simulated by Metropolis Monte Carlo with thermal cycles and quench experiments on 4^4 hypercubic lattices. The model has gauge coupling K and no matter (Higgs coupling h=0). The transition separates an electric confinement regime (small K) from an unconfined regime (large K). What is the ORDER of the bulk phase transition in the pure d=4 Z_2 lattice gauge theory? Answer in JSON with field "transition_order" using one of: "first_order", "continuous", "bkt".

metadata.system: Pure Z_2 lattice gauge theory on 4D hypercubic lattice, confinement-deconfinement transition
metadata.source_excerpt: The pure Z_2 lattice gauge theory on a four-dimensional hypercubic lattice with Higgs coupling set to h=0 exhibits a strongly first-order phase transition separating an electric confinement regime (Wilson loops obey an area law) for small gauge coupling K and an unconfined regime
metadata.difficulty_note: Clear strongly first-order result confirmed by hysteresis, metastability, and agreement with Creutz et al.

cold_solve.probe_model: claude-sonnet baseline (a cloud HPC platform sandbox for C1)
